claim_status: Plan only; no browser was launched by this manifest, no browser-reported version was captured, no profile was created, no benchmark ran, no competitor result exists, and no performance claim is supported.

allowed_reads: browser executable metadata already listed in the local-install manifest
the isolated temporary profile directory created for the capture run
runner-owned stdout, stderr, process-list, and artifact files
prohibited_reads: real Chrome, Edge, Firefox, Safari, or Safari Technology Preview user profile directories
browser history, cookies, credentials, bookmarks, downloads, sessions, cache, crash reports, sync data, account data, and extension data
personal files outside the runner-owned temporary artifact directory
prohibited_writes: source-controlled repository files except generated manifest updates reviewed by the operator
privacy_boundary.retention_policy: Temporary profiles are deleted after artifact hashing unless the operator explicitly keeps a redacted artifact bundle for review.

isolation_policy.profile_root_policy: Every browser launch uses a new runner-owned profile under %TEMP%\turing-browser-pin-capture\${run_id}\<browser>; the runner rejects existing user profile paths.
isolation_policy.profile_lifecycle: Create empty profile, launch for version/settings capture, collect artifacts, close browser, hash artifact index, delete profile by default.
isolation_policy.network_policy: Version capture is offline by default; any network access or browser-update check requires an explicit profiled network policy and owner approval.
isolation_policy.account_policy: No account, sync, password manager, credential store, or platform single-sign-on state may be attached to the capture profile.
isolation_policy.extension_policy: Extensions are disabled or absent unless an owner-approved extension workload explicitly records extension identity and state.
isolation_policy.update_policy: The capture records update state but must not trigger browser update, component update, or installation repair unless the owner has approved a separate update-control run.
isolation_policy.benchmark_argument_policy: Capture-only arguments used to prevent first-run, sync, or background network side effects are not automatically valid benchmark workload arguments.

browser_targets.planned_profile_mode: new temporary --user-data-dir under the runner-owned temp root
planned_version_capture: capture executable file/product version and SHA-256 from the local-install manifest
launch Chrome with the temporary profile and capture browser-reported version from a trusted version surface or automation endpoint
capture effective command line, profile path, update state, extension state, sync/account state, cache state, sandbox/site-isolation state, memory-saver state, and user-agent policy
expected_artifacts: browser-reported version text or structured automation output
blocked_until: runner implements timeout, close, cleanup, and artifact hashing
capture output proves the browser-reported version without using the owner profile

browser_targets.planned_profile_mode: new temporary --user-data-dir under the runner-owned temp root
planned_version_capture: capture executable file/product version and SHA-256 from the local-install manifest
launch Edge with the temporary profile and capture browser-reported version from a trusted version surface or automation endpoint
capture channel or update-ring proof because local executable metadata is newer than the recorded Stable release catalog

capture effective command line, profile path, update state, extension state, sync/account state, sidebar/workspaces state, cache state, sandbox/site-isolation state, memory-saver state, and user-agent policy
expected_artifacts: browser-reported version text or structured automation output
blocked_until: runner implements timeout, close, cleanup, and artifact hashing
capture output proves the browser-reported version without using the owner profile

browser_targets.planned_profile_mode: new temporary profile created by the runner once Firefox is installed on an approved host
planned_version_capture: launch Firefox with a temporary profile and capture browser-reported version from about:support or an approved automation endpoint
capture ETP, Fission/site-isolation, JIT, update, extension, sync/account, cache, user-agent, and command-line state
blocked_until: Firefox is installed on an approved benchmark host
owner approves Firefox temporary-profile capture method
runner implements timeout, close, cleanup, and artifact hashing

browser_targets.planned_profile_mode: new temporary macOS host-specific isolated user/profile strategy to be defined before capture
planned_version_capture: capture browser-reported version, Safari application version, and code-signature evidence on an approved macOS host
capture profile isolation, iCloud/account state, extension state, cache state, privacy settings, WebDriver state, power settings, and process settings
expected_artifacts: Safari application identity and version record

browser_targets.planned_profile_mode: new temporary macOS host-specific isolated preview-profile strategy to be defined before capture
planned_version_capture: capture browser-reported version, Safari Technology Preview application version, and code-signature evidence on an approved macOS host
capture explicit preview-channel label, profile isolation, extension state, cache state, WebDriver state, power settings, and process settings
expected_artifacts: Safari Technology Preview application identity and version record
blocked_until: Safari Technology Preview profile-isolation method is defined

artifact_contract: Every capture emits a manifest linking hardware_id, os_control_id, release_catalog_manifest_id, local_install_manifest_id, and capture_plan_id.
Every artifact has kind, path, byte size, and SHA-256.
Every browser target records whether the browser was launched, whether a temporary profile was created, whether cleanup succeeded, and whether any prohibited path was accessed.
Failed, timed-out, blocked, unsupported, and manually aborted captures remain in the denominator.
validation_contract: Reject any capture that uses a profile path under a real user browser profile.

Reject any capture that omits browser-reported version, effective command line, profile path, update state, extension state, cache state, sandbox/site-isolation state, or unsupported behavior once status moves beyond plan_no_browser_run.
Reject any benchmark comparison that uses capture-only arguments as workload arguments without owner review.
Reject any claim status other than no-claim until runner-generated benchmark artifacts and owner-reviewed comparison evidence exist.
unsupported_behavior: No browser was launched by this plan.
No temporary profile was created or hashed.
No command-line, settings, update, extension, sync/account, cache, sandbox, site-isolation, lifecycle, memory-saver, energy-saver, power, or user-agent output was captured.
No benchmark ran and no competitor comparison exists.
evidence_required: implemented browser-launch capture runner with timeout, cleanup, artifact hashing, and prohibited-path checks
owner-approved capture-only argument list for each browser
browser-reported version and effective settings capture for Chrome and Edge on Windows
Firefox local-install evidence on an approved Windows host
Safari and Safari Technology Preview local-install evidence on an approved macOS host
runner-generated benchmark manifest that references capture_plan_id and the resulting benchmark-ready browser pin IDs
raw artifacts and owner review before any competitor comparison or performance claim
